scenario: Aurora Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 with 12000 claims per day. After a hotfix in process definition v5 introduces an unintended branch, the platform team must move running process instances back to v3 while keeping their state. Separately, the integration team wants to relocate other running process instances onto a completely different process definition that consolidates two related products.
question: Which directions of process instance migration are supported between source and target process definitions?

options.text: Migration is allowed only from an older version to a strictly newer version of the same process definition with a matching bpmnProcessId
Migration is allowed between any versions of the same definition and to an entirely different process definition without a version restriction
Migration is allowed only between two adjacent versions of the same process definition when the version numbers differ by exactly one step
Migration is allowed only between two versions of the same process definition that share the same processDefinitionKey value across the deployments

a.text: Incorrect. The docs explicitly state that migration is supported from an older version to a newer version, from a newer version to an older version, and to a different process definition altogether.

b.text: Correct. The documented freedom is broad: a process instance may be migrated from older to newer, from newer to older within the same definition, or to a completely different process definition without further version constraints.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs impose no adjacency or step size on versions; any pair of versions of the same definition is allowed, and the target can also be a different definition.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs do not require the source and target to share the same processDefinitionKey; migration to a different process definition altogether is explicitly listed as supported.

explanation: Process instance migration places no direction or step restriction on version selection. The same definition can be migrated forward or backward across any versions, and the target can also be a completely different process definition with a different bpmnProcessId.
